evidence: Developing, testing, and deploying resource-intensive AI systems may require additional investment and increase the Companies costs.
Generative AI technologies are still in their early stages of development and deployment.
AI is an emerging area of technology that has the potential to impact various aspects of the Companies business operations and customer interactions.
Generative AI technologies are still in their early stages of development and deployment.
AI algorithms that the Companies or their third-party vendors use may be flawed or may be based on datasets that are biased or insufficient.

The rapidly evolving nature of AI technologies may cause new laws and regulations to be enacted which could dramatically affect business practices, including the costs to comply.

limitations: AI investment intensity: no named AI programs, dollar amounts, or headcount disclosed; only forward-looking cost acknowledgment present — scored 2 (generic statements).
AI narrative concreteness: excerpts contain only risk-section and aspirational language; no named deployed use cases or operational metrics found.

AI risk disclosure depth: excerpts are from the risk/business section and contain company-specific risk mechanisms (vendor opacity, algorithmic bias, regulatory cost, operational disruption), justifying score 4; no quantification or named governance body found to reach score 5.
